Rows: CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN T2.publisher_name = 'Marvel Comics' THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1.id)

gt_sql: SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN T2.publisher_name = 'Marvel Comics' THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1.id) FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN publisher AS T2 ON T1.publisher_id = T2.id INNER JOIN gender AS T3 ON T1.gender_id = T3.id WHERE T3.gender = 'Female'

Rows: CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN p.publisher_name = 'Marvel Comics' THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(s.id)

pred_sql:
SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN p.publisher_name = 'Marvel Comics' THEN 1 ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(s.id) 
FROM superhero s
JOIN gender g ON s.gender_id = g.id
JOIN publisher p ON s.publisher_id = p.id
WHERE g.gender = 'Female';
question: How many percent of female heroes were published by Marvel Comics?
